title: Redox OS has adopted a Certificate of Origin policy and a strict no-LLM policy
title_zh: Redox OS 已采用原产地证书政策及严格的禁止大型语言模型政策。
summary_zh: Redox OS项目近日宣布实施两项重要政策调整：一是采用来源证书（Certificate of Origin）政策，二是执行严格的禁止大型语言模型（no-LLM）政策。这些举措旨在强化项目的开源治理与代码质量保障。，来源证书政策要求所有代码贡献者在提交时明确声明其贡献内容的原创性及合规性，确认代码未侵犯第三方知识产权，并符合开源许可要求。这一机制有助于追溯代码来源，维护项目法律安全性，降低潜在版权风险。，而严格的no-LLM政策则明确规定，禁止使用任何大型语言模型（如GPT、Claude等AI生成工具）参与Redox OS的代码开发、文档编写或相关贡献。项目维护者认为，LLM生成的代码可能引入不可控的安全漏洞、逻辑错误或版权不清晰的代码片段，这与操作系统对稳定性、安全性和透明度的核心要求相悖。该政策旨在确保所有贡献均来自人类开发者，保障代码的可审计性和可靠性。，Redox OS是一个用Rust语言编写的开源操作系统，其设计注重安全性、并发性与模块化。此次政策调整反映了项目对长期可持续性与法律合规性的重视，同时也凸显了在AI工具日益普及的背景下，开源社区对代码质量与知识产权问题的新考量。这两项政策将作为项目贡献流程的强制规范，所有参与者均需遵守。

title: No, it doesn't cost Anthropic $5k per Claude Code user
content: No, it doesn't cost Anthropic $5k per Claude Code user - Martin Alderson My LinkedIn and Twitter feeds are full of screenshots from the recent Forbes article on Cursor claiming that Anthropic's $200/month Claude Code Max plan can consume $5,000 in compute. The relevant quote: Today, that subsidization appears to be even more aggressive, with that $200 plan able to consume about $5,000 in compute, according to a different person who has seen analyses on the company's compute spend patterns. This is being shared as proof that Anthropic is haemorrhaging money on inference. It doesn't survive basic scrutiny. What the $5,000 figure actually is I'm fairly confident the Forbes sources are confusing retail API prices with actual compute costs . These are very different things. Anthropic's current API pricing for Opus 4.6 is $5 per million input tokens and $25 per million output tokens. At those prices, yes - a heavy Claude Code Max 20 user could rack up $5,000/month in API-equivalent usage. That maths checks out. [1] But API pricing is not what it costs Anthropic to serve those tokens. The OpenRouter reality check The best way to estimate what inference actually costs is to look at what open-weight models of similar size are priced at on OpenRouter - where multiple providers compete on price. Qwen 3.5 397B-A17B is a good comparison point. It's a large MoE model, broadly comparable in architecture size to what Opus 4.6 is likely to be. Equally, so is Kimi K2.5 1T params with 32B active, which is probably approaching the upper limit of what you can efficiently serve. Here's what the pricing looks like: The Qwen 3.5 397B model on OpenRouter (via Alibaba Cloud) costs _$0.39_ per million input tokens and _$2.34_ per million output tokens. Compare that to Opus 4.6's API pricing of $5/$25. Kimi K2.5 is even cheaper at $0.45 per million input tokens and $2.25 output. That's roughly 10x cheaper . And this ratio holds for cached tokens too - DeepInfra charges $0.07/MTok for cache reads on Kimi K2.5 vs Anthropic's $0.50/MTok. These OpenRouter providers are running a business. They have to cover their compute costs, pay for GPUs, and make a margin. They're not charities. If so many can serve a model of comparable size at ~10% of Anthropic's API price and remain in business, it is hard for me to believe that they are all taking enormous losses (at ~the exact same rate range). If a heavy Claude Code Max user consumes $5,000 worth of tokens at Anthropic's retail API prices , and the actual compute cost is roughly 10% of that, Anthropic is looking at approximately $500 in real compute cost for the heaviest users. That's a loss of $300/month on the most extreme power users - not $4,800. However, most users don't come anywhere near the limit. Anthropic themselves said when they introduced weekly caps that fewer than 5% of subscribers would be affected . I personally use the Max 20x plan and probably consume around 50% of my weekly token budget and it's hard to use that ma
title_zh: 不，每位Claude Code用户并未让Anthropic花费5000美元
summary_zh: 近期《福布斯》关于Cursor的文章中声称，Anthropic每月200美元的Claude Code Max计划可能消耗高达5000美元的计算资源，这一说法在社交媒体上广泛传播。然而，这一数字经不起基本推敲。实际上，该数据很可能混淆了零售API价格与实际计算成本。，Anthropic目前对Opus 4.6的API定价为每百万输入token 5美元、每百万输出token 25美元。按照这一价格，重度Claude Code Max用户确实可能产生相当于5000美元的API使用量。但API定价并不等同于Anthropic提供这些token的实际成本。，通过OpenRouter平台上的开放权重模型定价可以更准确地估算推理成本。以架构规模与Opus 4.6相近的Qwen 3.5 397B-A17B模型为例，其在OpenRouter（通过阿里云提供）的定价为每百万输入token 0.39美元、输出token 2.34美元。相比之下，Opus 4.6的API价格高出约10倍。另一个可比模型Kimi K2.5的定价更为低廉，为每百万输入token 0.45美元、输出token 2.25美元。缓存token的定价差异同样显著：DeepInfra对Kimi K2.5的缓存读取收费为每百万token 0.07美元，而Anthropic的对应价格为0.50美元。，OpenRouter上的供应商需要覆盖计算成本、GPU费用并保持利润空间，他们并非慈善机构。如果多家供应商都能以Anthropic API价格约10%的水平提供规模相当的模型并持续运营，那么认为他们全部承受巨额亏损的说法难以成立。，因此，若一名重度Claude Code Max用户按照Anthropic零售API价格消耗了价值5000美元的token，实际计算成本可能仅为其10%左右，即约500美元。这意味着即使对极端重度用户，Anthropic每月可能损失约300美元，而非4800美元。此外，绝大多数用户远未达到使用上限。Anthropic在推出每周使用上限时曾表示，受影响用户比例不足5%。实际使用情况显示，多数用户仅消耗其token预算的一部分，远未达到理论极限值。

title: Is legal the same as legitimate: AI reimplementation and the erosion of copyleft
content: Is legal the same as legitimate: AI reimplementation and the erosion of copyleft — Hong Minhee on Things March 9, 2026 Is legal the same as legitimate: AI reimplementation and the erosion of copyleft Last week, Dan Blanchard, the maintainer of chardet—a Python library for detecting text encodings used by roughly 130 million projects a month— released a new version . Version 7.0 is 48 times faster than its predecessor, supports multiple cores, and was redesigned from the ground up. Anthropic's Claude is listed as a contributor. The license changed from LGPL to MIT. Blanchard's account is that he never looked at the existing source code directly. He fed only the API and the test suite to Claude and asked it to reimplement the library from scratch. The resulting code shares less than 1.3% similarity with any prior version, as measured by JPlag. His conclusion: this is an independent new work, and he is under no obligation to carry forward the LGPL. Mark Pilgrim, the library's original author, opened a GitHub issue to object . The LGPL requires that modifications be distributed under the same license, and a reimplementation produced with ample exposure to the original codebase cannot, in Pilgrim's view, pass as a clean-room effort. The dispute drew responses from two prominent figures in the open source world. Armin Ronacher, the creator of Flask, welcomed the relicensing . Salvatore Sanfilippo (antirez), the creator of Redis, published a broader defense of AI reimplementation , grounding it in copyright law and the history of the GNU project. Both conclude, by different routes, that what Blanchard did is legitimate. I respect both writers, and I think both are wrong—or more precisely, both are evading the question that actually matters. That question is this: does legal mean legitimate? Neither piece answers it. Both move from “this is legally permissible” to “this is therefore fine,” without pausing at the gap between those two claims. Law sets a floor; clearing it does not mean the conduct is right. That gap is where this essay begins. The analogy points the wrong way Antirez builds his case on history. When the GNU project reimplemented the UNIX userspace, it was lawful. So was Linux. Copyright law prohibits copying “protected expressions”—the actual code, its structure, its specific mechanisms—but it does not protect ideas or behavior. AI-assisted reimplementation occupies the same legal ground. Therefore, it is lawful. The legal analysis is largely correct, and I am not disputing it. The problem lies in what antirez does next: he presents the legal conclusion as if it were also a social one, and uses a historical analogy that, examined more carefully, argues against his own position. When GNU reimplemented the UNIX userspace, the vector ran from proprietary to free. Stallman was using the limits of copyright law to turn proprietary software into free software. The ethical force of that project did not come from its legal permissibility—it came
title_zh: 合法是否等同于正当：AI重实现与Copyleft的侵蚀
summary_zh: 2026年3月9日，Hong Minhee发表文章探讨AI重实现与Copyleft侵蚀问题，核心议题是“合法是否等同于正当”。争议源于Python文本编码检测库chardet的更新事件。该库维护者Dan Blanchard发布了7.0版本，性能提升48倍并支持多核，但将许可证从LGPL改为MIT。关键点在于，Blanchard称未直接查看原有源代码，而是仅向Anthropic的Claude提供API和测试套件，要求其从头重写库。经JPlag检测，新代码与旧版本相似度低于1.3%，因此他认为这是独立新作，无需继承LGPL条款。，原库作者Mark Pilgrim在GitHub提出异议，认为LGPL要求修改作品沿用相同许可，而充分接触原代码库后的AI重实现不能算作“净室工程”。开源社区两位知名人物对此回应：Flask创始人Armin Ronacher支持重新许可，Redis创始人Salvatore Sanfilippo（antirez）则从版权法和GNU项目历史出发，为AI重实现辩护，两人均认为Blanchard的做法正当。，作者指出，尽管双方从法律角度分析行为可能被允许，但均回避了核心问题：合法是否意味着正当？法律仅设定底线，符合法律不等于行为正确。antirez以GNU重实现UNIX用户空间的历史类比，主张AI重实现同样合法，因为版权法只保护“受保护的表达”（如具体代码结构），不保护思想或功能。作者承认这一法律分析基本正确，但指出类比方向错误：GNU项目是将专有软件转化为自由软件，其道德力量源于推动软件自由，而非单纯法律许可。而当前AI重实现可能将Copyleft软件转化为限制更少的许可，削弱了开源协作的伦理基础。这一争议揭示了在AI时代，传统Copyleft原则面临被技术手段绕过的风险，亟需社区重新审视合法性与正当性之间的界限。

title_zh: Yann LeCun的AI初创公司融资10亿美元，创欧洲史上最大种子轮融资纪录
summary_zh: 由AI先驱、图灵奖得主Yann LeCun联合创立的法国人工智能初创公司Kyutai，近日完成了超过10亿欧元的种子轮融资，这是欧洲有史以来规模最大的种子轮融资。本轮融资由法国亿万富翁Xavier Niel领投，其他主要投资者包括Rodolphe Saadé和Eric Schmidt。Kyutai定位为非营利性研究实验室，致力于推进人工智能的开放研究。，Kyutai的核心使命是开发先进的AI系统，特别是大型语言模型（LLM），并计划将研究成果开源。其研究重点在于追求“科学突破”，而不仅仅是商业应用。公司计划在巴黎设立一个研究实验室，并组建一支由约15名顶尖科学家组成的团队。，此次巨额融资凸显了欧洲在AI领域追赶美国的雄心，也反映了投资者对由Yann LeCun这样的学术巨擘领导的尖端AI基础研究的强烈信心。Kyutai的开源承诺，旨在促进AI技术的透明度和广泛可及性，与当前一些主流AI公司的闭源商业模式形成对比。该公司的成立和融资进展，标志着全球AI研究格局中一股重要的新力量正在欧洲崛起。

title: Learnings from paying artists royalties for AI-generated art
content: Learnings from Paying Artists Royalties for AI-Generated Art In 2024, we launched Tess.Design , a marketplace of fine-tuned AI image models where artists got paid a 50% royalty every time someone used their style. Less than two years later, we shut it down. This post is a candid account of what we built, what the data showed, and what any entrepreneur should know before trying to build an AI licensing business on top of creative talent. The Problem We Were Solving By 2023, AI image generation had become mainstream and deeply controversial. Image generation models like DALL-E and Midjourney trained on billions of scraped images, often without artist consent or compensation. Surveys consistently showed that consumers believed artists deserved payment when AI generated content in their style. Yet no business model existed to make that happen. Media companies were in a stalemate. Outlets like Rolling Stones and Fortune wanted AI-generated imagery for blogs, thumbnails, websites, and graphics. But their legal teams were nervous about copyright exposure, given the numerous ongoing lawsuits. So the publishers either banned AI tools entirely or turned a blind eye to their use, hoping for the best. Tess was our answer to this problem : an AI image generation platform where every image was traceable to a single consenting artist, who earned a royalty in its production. In this way, we argued that Tess was the first “properly-licensed” image generator that produced the same quality images as Midjourney and other leading models. How the Business Model Worked Tess Artists submitted a set of their work to fine-tune a Stable Diffusion base model. The resulting model, linked to that artist's aesthetic, was listed on a public marketplace at tess.design/models. Subscribers paid for access; the artist earned 50% of the revenue when subscribers used their model. The legal architecture mattered as much as the product. Working with Fenwick, we constructed a contributor agreement and Enterprise Service Agreement built on a novel copyright argument. We posited that because all outputs were stylistically transformed into the contributing artist's aesthetic, the artist held copyright over the derivative works and could license them downstream to customers.  This created a clean chain of copyright ownership, something no other AI image generator at the time offered. To seed the marketplace, we offered 25 founding artists an advance royalty of $300–$4,000 based on their portfolio quality and audience reach. In addition to income, we pitched artists on these value propositions: Passive income from royalties on every generation A dashboard showing exactly how their model was being used A free Tess subscription to use their own model for brainstorming and scaling repetitive work (roughly 1 in 4 artists took advantage of this) What 325 Cold Emails to Artists Taught Us In building Tess, we learned a lot from the growth path of Spotify, which took two years to develop its partner
title_zh: 为AI生成艺术向艺术家支付版税的启示
summary_zh: 2024年，我们推出了Tess.Design——一个专注于微调AI图像模型的市场平台。在该平台上，每当用户使用某位艺术家的风格生成图像时，该艺术家就能获得50%的版税分成。然而，运营不到两年后，我们关闭了这个项目。本文旨在坦诚分享我们的构建过程、数据揭示的真相，以及任何试图基于创意人才构建AI授权业务的创业者应提前了解的关键问题。，我们试图解决的问题，到2023年，AI图像生成已进入主流视野，同时也引发了巨大争议。DALL-E、Midjourney等模型基于数十亿张未经授权或补偿的抓取图像进行训练。多项调查显示，消费者普遍认为当AI以其风格生成内容时，艺术家理应获得报酬，但当时并无可行的商业模式来实现这一点。媒体公司也陷入两难：如《滚石》《财富》等机构希望将AI生成图像用于博客、缩略图、网站和图形设计，但其法务团队因面临大量未决诉讼而对版权风险深感担忧，导致出版商要么完全禁用AI工具，要么对其使用视而不见。，Tess正是我们对这一困境的回应：这是一个AI图像生成平台，确保每张生成图像都能追溯到一位已授权同意的艺术家，并由其在该图像的生成过程中获得版税。我们主张，Tess是首个“完全授权”的图像生成器，其输出质量与Midjourney及其他领先模型相当。，商业模式如何运作，艺术家提交一组自己的作品，用于对Stable Diffusion基础模型进行微调。生成的模型与该艺术家的美学风格关联，并公开列在tess.design/models市场上。订阅者付费使用模型；当订阅者使用某位艺术家的模型时，该艺术家可获得50%的收入分成。，法律架构与产品本身同等重要。在与Fenwick律师事务所合作下，我们基于一项新颖的版权论点，构建了贡献者协议和企业服务协议。我们主张，由于所有输出在风格上都转化为贡献艺术家的美学表达，因此艺术家对衍生作品持有版权，并可向下游客户授权。这形成了一条清晰的版权所有权链，这是当时其他AI图像生成器均未提供的。，为启动市场，我们根据作品集质量和受众影响力，向25位创始艺术家提供了300至4000美元不等的预付版税。除了收入，我们还向艺术家强调了以下价值主张：每次生成带来的版税被动收入、展示模型使用详情的仪表板，以及可免费使用Tess订阅（利用自有模型进行头脑风暴和规模化重复性工作，约四分之一的艺术家利用了此功能）。，从325封致艺术家的冷邮件中获得的启示，在构建Tess的过程中，我们从Spotify的发展路径汲取了许多经验。

title: Yann LeCun Raises $1B to Build AI That Understands the Physical World
content: Yann LeCun Raises $1 Billion to Build AI That Understands the Physical World | WIRED Skip to main content Save Story Save this story Save Story Save this story Advanced Machine Intelligence (AMI), a new Paris-based startup cofounded by Meta’s former chief AI scientist Yann LeCun , announced Monday it has raised more than $1 billion to develop AI world models . LeCun argues that most human reasoning is grounded in the physical world, not language, and that AI world models are necessary to develop true human-level intelligence. “The idea that you’re going to extend the capabilities of LLMs [large language models] to the point that they’re going to have human-level intelligence is complete nonsense,” he said in an interview with WIRED. The financing, which values the startup at $3.5 billion, was co-led by investors such as Cathay Innovation, Greycroft, Hiro Capital, HV Capital, and Bezos Expeditions. Other notable backers include Mark Cuban , former Google CEO Eric Schmidt , and French billionaire and telecommunications executive Xavier Niel. AMI (pronounced like the French word for friend) aims to build “a new breed of AI systems that understand the world, have persistent memory, can reason and plan, and are controllable and safe,” the company says in a press release. The startup says it will be global from day one, with offices in Paris, Montreal, Singapore, and New York, where LeCun will continue working as a New York University professor in addition to leading the startup. AMI will be the first commercial endeavor for LeCun since his departure from Meta in November 2025. LeCun’s startup represents a bet against many of the world’s biggest AI labs like OpenAI, Anthropic, and even his former workplace, Meta, which believe that scaling up LLMs will eventually deliver AI systems with human-level intelligence or even superintelligence. LLMs have powered viral products such as ChatGPT and Claude Code, but LeCun has been one of the AI industry’s most prominent researchers speaking out about the limitations of these AI models. LeCun is well known for being outspoken , but as a pioneer of modern AI that won a Turing award back in 2018, his skepticism carries weight. LeCun says AMI aims to work with companies in manufacturing, biomedical, robotics, and other industries that have lots of data. For example, he says AMI could build a realistic world model of an aircraft engine and work with the manufacturer to help them optimize for efficiency, minimize emissions, or ensure reliability. AMI was cofounded by LeCun and several leaders he worked with at Meta, including the company’s former director of research science, Michael Rabbat; former vice president of Europe, Laurent Solly; and former senior director of AI research, Pascale Fung. Other cofounders include Alexandre LeBrun, former CEO of the AI health care startup Nabla, who will serve as AMI’s CEO, and Saining Xie, a former Google DeepMind researcher who will be the startup’s chief science officer. The C
title_zh: Yann LeCun 筹集10亿美元打造理解物理世界的人工智能
summary_zh: 由Meta前首席AI科学家Yann LeCun联合创立的巴黎初创公司Advanced Machine Intelligence（AMI）近日宣布，已融资超过10亿美元用于开发AI世界模型。该公司估值达35亿美元，本轮融资由凯辉基金、Greycroft、Hiro Capital、HV Capital和贝索斯旗下Bezos Expeditions等机构共同领投，其他知名投资者包括马克·库班、谷歌前CEO埃里克·施密特以及法国电信业亿万富翁泽维尔·尼尔。，LeCun在采访中强调，人类大部分推理基于物理世界而非语言，因此AI世界模型是实现人类水平智能的关键。他直言仅靠扩展大型语言模型（LLM）达到人类智能是“完全无稽之谈”，这与OpenAI、Anthropic及Meta等主流AI实验室的发展路径形成鲜明对比。作为2018年图灵奖得主，LeCun的观点在业内具有重要影响力。，AMI旨在构建“新一代能理解世界、具备持久记忆、可推理规划、且可控安全的人工智能系统”。公司计划在巴黎、蒙特利尔、新加坡和纽约设立全球办公室，LeCun除领导公司外将继续担任纽约大学教授。这是LeCun于2025年11月离开Meta后的首次商业尝试。，核心技术方面，AMI将专注于为制造业、生物医学、机器人等数据密集型行业构建物理世界模型。例如通过建立飞机发动机的高保真世界模型，帮助制造商优化效率、减少排放并确保可靠性。公司联合创始人团队包括Meta前研究科学总监Michael Rabbat、欧洲业务前副总裁Laurent Solly、AI研究前高级总监Pascale Fung，以及AI医疗初创公司Nabla前CEO Alexandre LeBrun（现任AMI CEO）、谷歌DeepMind前研究员Saining Xie（现任首席科学官）。，此次融资标志着AI领域出现了一条区别于LLM规模扩张的新技术路线，其核心价值在于通过物理世界建模实现更接近人类认知方式的推理能力。

title: msitarzewski/agency-agents - A complete AI agency at your fingertips - From frontend wizards to Reddit community ninjas, from whimsy injectors to reality checkers. Each agent is a specialized expert with personality, processes, and proven deliverables.

Each agent is a specialized expert with personality, processes, and proven deliverables. · GitHub Skip to content You signed in with another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. Dismiss alert msitarzewski / agency-agents Public Notifications You must be signed in to change notification settings Fork 3.5k Star 22.1k main Branches Tags Go to file Code Open more actions menu Folders and files Name Name Last commit message Last commit date Latest commit History 92 Commits 92 Commits .github .github design design engineering engineering examples examples integrations integrations marketing marketing product product project-management project-management scripts scripts spatial-computing spatial-computing specialized specialized strategy strategy support support testing testing .gitattributes .gitattributes .gitignore .gitignore CONTRIBUTING.md CONTRIBUTING.md LICENSE LICENSE README.md README.md View all files Repository files navigation 🎭 The Agency: AI Specialists Ready to Transform Your Workflow A complete AI agency at your fingertips - From frontend wizards to Reddit community ninjas, from whimsy injectors to reality checkers. Each agent is a specialized expert with personality, processes, and proven deliverables. 🚀 What Is This? Born from a Reddit thread and months of iteration, The Agency is a growing collection of meticulously crafted AI agent personalities. Each agent is: 🎯 Specialized : Deep expertise in their domain (not generic prompt templates) 🧠 Personality-Driven : Unique voice, communication style, and approach 📋 Deliverable-Focused : Real code, processes, and measurable outcomes ✅ Production-Ready : Battle-tested workflows and success metrics Think of it as : Assembling your dream team, except they're AI specialists who never sleep, never complain, and always deliver. ⚡ Quick Start Option 1: Use with Claude Code (Recommended) # Copy agents to your Claude Code directory cp -r agency-agents/ * ~ /.claude/agents/ # Now activate any agent in your Claude Code sessions: # "Hey Claude, activate Frontend Developer mode and help me build a React component" Option 2: Use as Reference Each agent file contains: Identity & personality traits Core mission & workflows Technical deliverables with code examples Success metrics & communication style Browse the agents below and copy/adapt the ones you need! Option 3: Use with Other Tools (Cursor, Aider, Windsurf, Gemini CLI, OpenCode) # Step 1 -- generate integration files for all supported tools ./scripts/convert.sh # Step 2 -- install interactively (auto-detects what you have installed) ./scripts/install.sh # Or target a specific tool directly ./scripts/install.sh --tool curs
title_zh: msitarzewski/agency-agents - 触手可及的完整AI代理机构 - 从前端魔法师到Reddit社区忍者，从奇思妙想注入者到现实检验员。每个代理都是具备个性、流程和已验证交付成果的专业专家。
summary_zh: GitHub项目“msitarzewski/agency-agents”提供了一个完整的AI专家团队集合，旨在彻底改变用户的工作流程。该项目将一系列精心设计的AI智能体人格化，每个智能体都是拥有独特个性、专业流程和已验证交付成果的领域专家。其覆盖范围极广，从前端开发向导到Reddit社区运营专家，从创意灵感注入者到现实核查员，应有尽有。，该项目的核心价值在于其高度专业化与实用性。每个AI智能体并非通用的提示模板，而是在特定领域具备深厚专业知识的“专家”。它们拥有独特的沟通风格和明确的工作流程，专注于产出可衡量的实际成果，如真实可用的代码、标准化流程等，并且所有工作流都经过实战检验，具备生产就绪性。，用户可以通过多种方式快速启用这些AI专家。推荐的方式是与Claude Code集成，只需简单地将智能体文件复制到指定目录，即可在会话中激活并使用，例如直接请求“激活前端开发者模式来帮助构建React组件”。此外，用户也可以将智能体文件作为参考模板，直接浏览、复制或适配所需的部分。项目还提供了强大的脚本工具，支持与其他主流开发工具（如Cursor、Aider、Windsurf、Gemini CLI、OpenCode等）进行集成，通过运行转换和安装脚本即可实现一键部署。，简而言之，该项目如同一个永不疲倦、随时待命的梦幻专家团队，为用户提供了触手可及的、模块化的AI能力，能够显著提升从产品开发、工程设计到市场营销、项目管理等各个环节的效率与专业性。

It's the only agent with a built-in learning loop — it creates skills from experience, improves them during use, nudges itself to persist knowledge, searches its own past conversations, and builds a deepening model of who you are across sessions. Run it on a $5 VPS, a GPU cluster, or serverless infrastructure that costs nearly nothing when idle. It's not tied to your laptop — talk to it from Telegram while it works on a cloud VM. Use any model you want — Nous Portal , OpenRouter (200+ models), z.ai/GLM , Kimi/Moonshot , MiniMax , OpenAI, or your own endpoint. Switch with hermes model — no code changes, no lock-in. A real terminal interface Full TUI with multiline editing, slash-command autocomplete, conversation history, interrupt-and-redirect, and streaming tool output. Lives where you do Telegram, Discord, Slack, WhatsApp, Signal, and CLI — all from a single gateway process. Voice memo transcription, cross-platform conversation continuity. A closed learning loop Agent-curated memory with perio
title_zh: NousResearch/hermes-agent - 与你共同成长的智能体
summary_zh: NousResearch在GitHub上开源了Hermes Agent项目，这是一个具备自我进化能力的AI智能体。其核心创新在于内置了完整的学习循环机制：智能体能够从过往经验中自主创建技能，并在使用过程中持续优化这些技能；它会主动推动自身保存知识，能够搜索历史对话记录，并在多次交互会话中逐步构建并深化对用户的理解模型。，该项目设计注重实用性与灵活性。在部署方面，它支持从成本低廉的VPS、GPU集群到闲置时费用极低的无服务器架构等多种基础设施。智能体独立运行，用户可通过Telegram等应用与部署在云端的智能体进行交互，无需绑定本地机器。在模型支持上，它兼容众多主流及自定义模型端点，包括Nous Portal、OpenRouter（支持200多个模型）、z.ai/GLM、Kimi/Moonshot、MiniMax、OpenAI等，用户可通过`hermes model`命令自由切换，无需修改代码，避免了供应商锁定。，Hermes Agent提供了功能完整的终端用户界面，支持多行编辑、斜杠命令自动补全、对话历史查看、中断与重定向操作以及流式工具输出。它通过一个统一的网关进程，集成了Telegram、Discord、Slack、WhatsApp、Signal和CLI等多种通信平台，实现了跨平台的对话连续性，并支持语音备忘录转录功能。，其关键技术架构实现了封闭的学习循环，包含由智能体管理的记忆系统、周期性重放机制、技能提炼与压缩过程，并能生成用于微调的训练数据。项目提供了多种运行模式：基础对话模式、具备学习循环的“学习模式”、用于评估的“基准测试模式”，以及一个专门用于解决GitHub问题的“代码代理模式”。项目还包含了用于技能开发的工具包、模拟训练环境，并计划推出图形用户界面和技能市场。

title_zh: promptfoo/promptfoo - 测试您的提示词、智能体和RAG系统。面向大语言模型的红队测试、渗透测试与漏洞扫描。对比GPT、Claude、Gemini、Llama等模型的性能表现。采用简洁声明式配置，支持命令行与CI/CD集成。
summary_zh: promptfoo是一个用于评估和红队测试LLM应用程序的CLI工具与库。该项目旨在帮助开发者摆脱反复试错的传统方式，从而构建出安全、可靠的人工智能应用。其核心功能包括测试提示词（prompts）、智能体（agents）和检索增强生成（RAG）系统，并提供针对LLM的AI红队测试、渗透测试和漏洞扫描能力。，该工具的一个关键特性是能够横向比较不同大型语言模型的性能，支持包括GPT、Claude、Gemini、Llama在内的多种主流模型。用户可以通过简单的声明式配置文件来定义测试用例和评估标准，并集成命令行界面以及CI/CD流程，实现自动化测试。，项目提供了便捷的安装方式，例如通过npm进行全局安装（npm install -g promptfoo），也支持brew、pip等包管理器。用户可以通过初始化示例项目（promptfoo init --example getting-started）快速上手。其架构涵盖了从配置、插件、示例到完整源代码的多个模块，便于扩展和定制。，总体而言，promptfoo为LLM应用的开发与部署提供了系统化的质量保障框架，通过标准化测试和安全性评估，助力团队提升AI输出的可靠性与安全性。

Here are the latest notebooks and demos using the new model: Intro to Gemini 3.1 Pro This repository contains notebooks, code samples, sample apps, and other resources that demonstrate how to use, develop and manage generative AI workflows using Generative AI on Google Cloud with Vertex AI . Using this repository Description gemini/ Discover Gemini through starter notebooks, use cases, function calling, sample apps, and more. search/ Use this folder if you're interested in using Vertex AI Search , a Google-managed solution to help you rapidly build search engines for websites and across enterprise data. (Formerly known as Enterprise Search on Generative AI App Builder). rag-grounding/ Use this folder for information on Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) and Grounding with Vertex AI. This is an index of notebooks and samples across other directories focused on this topic. vision/ Use this folder if you're interested in building your own solutions from scratch using features from Imagen on Vertex AI (Vertex AI Imagen API). These are the features that Imagen on Vertex AI offers: Image generation Image editing Visual captioning Visual question answering audio/ Use this folder if you're interested in building your own solutions from scratch using features from Chirp, a version of Google's Univers
title_zh: GoogleCloudPlatform/generative-ai - 谷歌云生成式AI示例代码与笔记本，基于Vertex AI平台的Gemini模型
summary_zh: 谷歌云平台在其GitHub官方仓库“GoogleCloudPlatform/generative-ai”中，发布了用于在谷歌云上进行生成式AI开发的示例代码和Notebook，重点集成了Vertex AI平台上的Gemini模型。该仓库资源丰富，旨在帮助开发者学习、开发和管理基于谷歌云的生成式AI工作流。，仓库的核心内容围绕Gemini系列模型展开，特别强调了新发布的Gemini 3.1 Pro，并提供了相应的介绍性Notebook和演示。主要功能模块包括：，“gemini/”目录：包含入门Notebook、用例演示、函数调用示例以及示例应用程序，是探索Gemini模型功能的主要区域。，“search/”目录：专注于Vertex AI Search（原Generative AI App Builder中的企业搜索），这是一个谷歌托管的解决方案，可帮助快速为网站和企业数据构建搜索引擎。，“rag-grounding/”目录：提供关于检索增强生成（RAG）和Vertex AI基础（Grounding）的信息，汇集了与此主题相关的Notebook和样本索引。，“vision/”目录：专注于使用Vertex AI上的Imagen API（Vertex AI Imagen）从头构建视觉解决方案，其功能涵盖图像生成、图像编辑、视觉描述（字幕生成）和视觉问答。，“audio/”目录：涉及使用谷歌通用语音模型Chirp的版本来构建音频相关解决方案。，此外，仓库还包含其他资源，如用于智能体开发的“agents/”、模型嵌入“embeddings/”、开发框架“genkit”、开源模型“open-models”、合作伙伴模型“partner-models”、SDK工具以及评估工具包“tools/llmevalkit”等。，该项目为开发者提供了一个实践性的起点，通过结构化的代码示例和Notebook，降低了在谷歌云Vertex AI平台上利用Gemini等先进模型进行生成式AI应用开发的门槛。

Dismiss alert virattt / ai-hedge-fund Public Notifications You must be signed in to change notification settings Fork 8.2k Star 47.3k main Branches Tags Go to file Code Open more actions menu Folders and files Name Name Last commit message Last commit date Latest commit History 803 Commits 803 Commits .github/ ISSUE_TEMPLATE .github/ ISSUE_TEMPLATE app app docker docker src src tests tests .dockerignore .dockerignore .env.example .env.example .gitignore .gitignore README.md README.md poetry.lock poetry.lock pyproject.toml pyproject.toml View all files Repository files navigation AI Hedge Fund This is a proof of concept for an AI-powered hedge fund. The goal of this project is to explore the use of AI to make trading decisions. This project is for educational purposes only and is not intended for real trading or investment. This system employs several agents working together: Aswath Damodaran Agent - The Dean of Valuation, focuses on story, numbers, and disciplined valuation Ben Graham Agent - The godfather of value investing, only buys hidden gems with a margin of safety Bill Ackman Agent - An activist investor, takes bold positions and pushes for change Cathie Wood Agent - The queen of growth investing, believes in the power of innovation and disruption Charlie Munger Agent - Warren Buffett's partner, only buys wonderful businesses at fair prices Michael Burry Agent - The Big Short contrarian who hunts for deep value Mohnish Pabrai Agent - The Dhandho investor, who looks for doubles at low risk Peter Lynch Agent - Practical investor who seeks "ten-baggers" in everyday businesses Phil Fisher Agent - Meticulous growth investor who uses deep "scuttlebutt" research Rakesh Jhunjhunwala Agent - The Big Bull of India Stanley Druckenmiller Agent - Macro legend who hunts for asymmetric opportunities with growth potential Warren Buffett Agent - The oracle of Omaha, seeks wonderful companies at a fair price Valuation Agent - Calculates the intrinsic value of a stock and generates trading signals Sentiment Agent - Analyzes market sentiment and generates trading signals Fundamentals Agent - Analyzes fundamental data and generates trading signals Technicals Agent - Analyzes technical indicators and generates trading signals Risk Manager - Calculates risk metrics and sets position limits Portfolio Manager - Makes final trading decisions and generates orders Note: the system does not actually make any trades. Disclaimer This project is for educational and research purposes only . Not intended for real trading or investment No investment advice or guarantees provided Creator assumes no liability for financial losses Consult a financial advisor for investment dec
title_zh: Virattt/ai-hedge-fund - 一支人工智能对冲基金团队
summary_zh: GitHub 开源项目 "virattt/ai-hedge-fund" 是一个概念验证性质的 AI 对冲基金系统。该项目旨在探索利用人工智能进行交易决策，但明确声明仅用于教育研究目的，不涉及真实交易或投资，也不提供任何投资建议或保证。，该系统的核心设计是模拟一个由多个具备不同投资风格的 AI Agent 协同工作的团队。每个 Agent 都以一位著名投资大师的理念为原型，负责特定维度的分析并生成交易信号。具体包括：，* 价值投资型 Agent：如 Warren Buffett Agent（寻求以合理价格投资卓越公司）、Charlie Munger Agent、Ben Graham Agent（寻找具有安全边际的隐藏瑰宝）和 Michael Burry Agent（逆向寻找深度价值）。，* 成长投资型 Agent：如 Cathie Wood Agent（相信创新与颠覆的力量）、Phil Fisher Agent（通过细致调研寻找成长股）。，* 其他风格 Agent：如 Peter Lynch Agent（从日常生活中寻找“十倍股”）、Stanley Druckenmiller Agent（宏观策略，寻找不对称机会）、Rakesh Jhunjhunwala Agent、Bill Ackman Agent（积极主义投资）和 Aswath Damodaran Agent（专注估值）。，* 专业分析 Agent：包括负责计算股票内在价值的 Valuation Agent、分析市场情绪的 Sentiment Agent、分析基本面数据的 Fundamentals Agent 以及分析技术指标的 Technicals Agent。，此外，系统还设有 Risk Manager（计算风险指标并设置头寸限制）和 Portfolio Manager（做出最终交易决策并生成指令）来管理整体流程。需要再次强调的是，该系统仅为模拟，不会执行任何真实交易。项目结构包含应用代码、Docker 配置、测试及依赖管理文件，为开发者提供了一个研究多智能体 AI 在金融投资领域应用的框架。

Control web interfaces with natural language. 🌐 English | 中文 👉 🚀 Demo | 📖 Documentation | 📢 Join HN Discussion page-agent-demo-0227.mp4 ✨ Features 🎯 Easy integration No need for browser extension / python / headless browser . Just in-page javascript. Everything happens in your web page. The best tool for your agent to control web pages. 📖 Text-based DOM manipulation No screenshots. No OCR or multi-modal LLMs needed. No special permissions required. 🧠 Bring your own LLMs 🎨 Pretty UI with human-in-the-loop 🐙 Optional chrome extension for multi-page tasks. 💡 Use Cases SaaS AI Copilot — Ship an AI copilot in your product in lines of code. No backend rewrite needed. Smart Form Filling — Turn 20-click workflows into one sentence. Perfect for ERP, CRM, and admin systems. Accessibility — Make any web app accessible through natural language. Voice commands, screen readers, zero barrier. Multi-page Agent — Extend your agent's reach across browser tabs with the optional chrome extension . 🚀 Quick Start One-line integration Fastest way to try PageAgent with our free Demo LLM: < script src =" {URL} " crossorigin =" true " > </ script > Mirrors URL Global https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/page-agent@1.5.4/dist/iife/page-agent.demo.js China https://registry.npmmirror.com/page-agent/1.5.4/files/dist/iife/page-agent.demo.js ⚠️ For technical evaluation only. This demo CDN uses our free testing LLM API . By using it, you agree to its terms . NPM Installation npm install page-agent import { PageAgent } from 'page-agent' const agent = new PageAgent ( { model : 'qwen3.5-plus' , baseURL : 'https://dashscope.aliyuncs.com/compatible-mode/v1' , apiKey : 'YOUR_API_KEY' , language : 'en-US' , } ) await agent . execute ( 'Click the login button' ) For more programmatic usage, see 📖 Documentations . 🤝 Contributing We welcome contributions from the community! Follow our instructions in CONTRIBU
title_zh: 阿里巴巴/page-agent - JavaScript 页面图形用户界面代理。用自然语言控制网页界面。
summary_zh: 阿里巴巴开源项目 page-agent 是一款基于 JavaScript 的网页内 GUI 智能体，允许用户通过自然语言直接控制网页界面。该项目旨在简化网页自动化操作，无需依赖浏览器扩展、Python 脚本或无头浏览器，所有交互均在当前网页内通过 JavaScript 完成。，核心功能与特点包括：易于集成，开发者只需在网页中引入一行脚本即可快速部署；采用基于文本的 DOM 操作，无需截图、OCR 或多模态 LLM，也无需特殊权限；支持用户自带 LLM（如 GPT、Claude 等）作为后端模型；提供美观的交互界面，支持人在回路的操作模式；并提供了一个可选的 Chrome 扩展，以支持跨浏览器标签页的多页面任务处理。，其应用场景广泛：可作为 SaaS 产品的 AI Copilot，仅需少量代码即可为产品嵌入智能辅助功能，无需重写后端；能实现智能表单填写，将复杂的多步骤操作简化为一句自然语言指令，适用于 ERP、CRM 等管理系统；可提升网络应用的无障碍访问能力，通过自然语言或语音命令进行操作；借助 Chrome 扩展，还能构建可跨页面工作的智能体。，项目提供了快速入门方式。用户可通过 CDN 一键集成体验版，或通过 NPM 安装进行更深入的编程集成。体验版使用了项目方提供的免费测试 LLM API，仅建议用于技术评估。对于正式使用，开发者需要配置自己的 LLM API（如通义千问等）和密钥。，该项目已在 GitHub 上开源，拥有活跃的社区，并欢迎开发者贡献代码。

title_zh: openclaw/openclaw - 您的专属AI助手。跨操作系统。跨平台。龙虾之道。🦞
summary_zh: OpenClaw是一个可在本地设备上运行的个性化AI助手项目，遵循“龙虾方式”的理念，旨在提供跨操作系统和平台的灵活部署方案。该项目在GitHub上开源，拥有活跃的社区贡献。，核心功能上，OpenClaw允许用户通过本地部署获得一个私有的AI助手，能够直接响应用户需求。其架构支持模块化扩展，包含技能（skills）、扩展（extensions）和应用（apps）等组件，用户可以根据需要定制功能。项目提供了完整的开发工具链，包括测试配置、Docker支持以及持续集成/部署（CI/CD）工作流，便于开发和集成。，技术层面，项目采用现代Web技术栈，使用TypeScript开发，并通过pnpm管理多包工作区。它包含详尽的配置项，支持从本地测试到容器化部署的多种场景。项目文件结构清晰，涵盖了核心源代码、文档、测试用例以及供应商代码，体现了工程化的项目管理。，该项目强调自主控制和隐私保护，用户数据无需上传至云端。通过提供Docker镜像和详细的部署指南（如docker-compose.yml），它简化了在任意环境中的安装与运行过程。OpenClaw的设计目标是成为一个高度可定制、不依赖特定云服务的个人AI基础设施，为开发者构建专属AI助手提供了坚实基础。

title: pbakaus/impeccable - The design language that makes your AI harness better at design.
content: GitHub - pbakaus/impeccable: The design language that makes your AI harness better at design. · GitHub Skip to content You signed in with another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. You signed out in another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. You switched accounts on another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. Dismiss alert pbakaus / impeccable Public Notifications You must be signed in to change notification settings Fork 124 Star 3.4k main Branches Tags Go to file Code Open more actions menu Folders and files Name Name Last commit message Last commit date Latest commit History 124 Commits 124 Commits .claude-plugin .claude-plugin .claude/ skills .claude/ skills functions/ api/ download functions/ api/ download public public scripts scripts server server source/ skills source/ skills tests tests .gitignore .gitignore AGENTS.md AGENTS.md CLAUDE.md CLAUDE.md DEVELOP.md DEVELOP.md LICENSE LICENSE NOTICE.md NOTICE.md README.md README.md biome.json biome.json bun.lock bun.lock package.json package.json wrangler.toml wrangler.toml View all files Repository files navigation Impeccable The vocabulary you didn't know you needed. 1 skill, 17 commands, and curated anti-patterns for impeccable frontend design. Quick start: Visit impeccable.style to download ready-to-use bundles. Why Impeccable? Anthropic created frontend-design , a skill that guides Claude toward better UI design. Impeccable builds on that foundation with deeper expertise and more control. Every LLM learned from the same generic templates. Without guidance, you get the same predictable mistakes: Inter font, purple gradients, cards nested in cards, gray text on colored backgrounds. Impeccable fights that bias with: An expanded skill with 7 domain-specific reference files ( view source ) 17 steering commands to audit, review, polish, distill, animate, and more Curated anti-patterns that explicitly tell the AI what NOT to do What's Included The Skill: frontend-design A comprehensive design skill with 7 domain-specific references ( view skill ): Reference Covers typography Type systems, font pairing, modular scales, OpenType color-and-contrast OKLCH, tinted neutrals, dark mode, accessibility spatial-design Spacing systems, grids, visual hierarchy motion-design Easing curves, staggering, reduced motion interaction-design Forms, focus states, loading patterns responsive-design Mobile-first, fluid design, container queries ux-writing Button labels, error messages, empty states 17 Commands Command What it does /teach-impeccable One-time setup: gather design context, save to config /audit Run technical quality checks (a11y, performance, responsive) /critique UX design review: hierarchy, clarity, emotional resonance /normalize Align with design system standards /polish Final pass before shipping /distill Strip to essence /clarify Improve unclear UX copy /optimize Performance improvements /harden Error handling, i18n, edge cases /animate Add purposeful motion /coloriz
title_zh: pbakaus/impeccable - 让您的AI设计工具更精于设计的设计语言。
summary_zh: GitHub用户pbakaus近日发布了名为"Impeccable"的开源项目，这是一个旨在提升AI设计能力的设计语言体系。该项目基于Anthropic公司开发的frontend-design技能进行扩展，通过提供结构化设计词汇和约束规则，引导Claude等LLM生成更专业的前端设计方案。，核心功能包含三大组成部分：首先是经过扩展的frontend-design技能，内置7个专业设计领域的参考文件，涵盖字体排印（Type systems、OpenType特性）、色彩对比（OKLCH色彩空间、无障碍标准）、空间设计（间距系统、视觉层次）、动效设计（缓动曲线、交错动画）、交互设计（表单状态、焦点管理）、响应式设计（移动优先、容器查询）以及UX文案（按钮标签、错误提示）等现代前端设计的关键维度。，其次提供了17条精准的操控指令，包括/audit（运行技术质量审查）、/critique（UX设计评审）、/normalize（设计系统对齐）、/polish（交付前优化）、/distill（提炼设计本质）、/animate（添加动效）等标准化工作流程命令。这些指令使开发者能够系统化地指导AI完成从设计审计到细节优化的全流程。，项目特别针对LLM常见设计偏见提出解决方案，通过明确定义的设计反模式库，直接告知AI应避免的典型错误设计模式，如过度使用Inter字体、紫色渐变、卡片嵌套、彩色背景上的灰色文字等泛滥的模板化设计倾向。所有资源可通过impeccable.style网站获取即用型工具包，支持Claude插件集成和API调用，为AI辅助设计工作流提供了标准化、可复用的专业设计约束框架。
